title: About — the AIAD framework and its author
desc: AIAD is an open-source (MIT) meta-framework created by Steeve Evers: it governs human-AI teamwork, with SDD Mode as its operating mode. Positioning against Kiro, Spec-Kit, SPDD and Symphony, and a presentation of this site.

sections.t: This site, a demonstrator of the framework

title: The artifacts — the team's collective memory
desc: AIAD artifacts are not bureaucracy: the Intent Statement, PRD, ARCHITECTURE, AGENT-GUIDE, SPECs and Definitions of Done form a strict documentation hierarchy — the team's collective memory and the fuel of AI agents.

title: Big Picture — The interactive SDD Mode cycle
desc: The SDD Mode Big Picture: an interactive diagram of the full cycle — Intent, Research, SPEC, Execution Gate, agent execution, validation, Drift Lock — with Tier 1 governance, artifacts, responsibilities and metrics. Every element is clickable.

title: /aiad — rituals, synchronisations and metrics
desc: Complete reference for the /aiad router: init, onboard, status, health, gouvernance, tech-review, standup, demo, retro, intention, sync-strat, dora, flow, dashboard, dashboard-html, emit-rules. Team rituals, synchronisations and metrics of the AIAD framework.

sections.t: /aiad init — bootstrap on an existing project

title: npx aiad-sdd CLI — install and tool up
desc: The aiad-sdd command line: init (.aiad/ structure, governance, slash commands), update, status, trace --fail-on-gap, multi-runtime emit-rules, exit codes 0/1/2, GitHub Actions CI integrations and hooks (pre-commit, session-start, discovery-gate).

title: /sdd — the 16 cycle commands
desc: Complete reference for the /sdd router: init, prd, arch, intent, research, spec, gate, exec, resume, split, context, validate, drift-check, trace, fact, security, audit. For each command: when to use it, what it does, its output and the skills it invokes.

title: Context Engineering Budget — the discipline of context
desc: The Context Engineering Budget is principle #3 of SDD Mode: the Product Engineer owns the context budget of every agent session. 60-70% operational threshold, a single active SPEC, the Intent at the top of the context, 5 health metrics M1-M5.

sections.t: Context rot — degradation before the limit
The operational threshold: 60-70% of the window
The context-budget skill and the 5 health metrics

title: Drift Lock — code and SPEC in the same PR
desc: Drift Lock treats spec drift as a process failure: code and SPEC synchronized in the same PR, a 3-question Anti-Drift Check, algorithmic measurement via /sdd trace and the sdd-trace.yml GitHub Action (--fail-on-gap), and an UNKNOWN verdict when @spec annotations are missing.

sections.t: A process failure, not an agent error
The Anti-Drift Check — three questions at the end of the iteration
Algorithmic measurement (v1.10) — /sdd trace and the CI
UNKNOWN verdict — absence of evidence is not evidence
The artifact Sync micro-phase — 15 minutes, 4 questions

title: Execution Gate — SQS ≥ 4/5 or nothing
desc: The Execution Gate is the checkpoint between a SPEC and launching the agent: 5 SQS criteria, the Stranger Test, strict EARS mode, fail-closed UNKNOWN Gate (JNSP) and Context Engineering Budget preparation. No code before the Gate is open.

sections.t: Why a gate before the code
The five Spec Quality Score criteria

title: Agent Execution — orchestrate without saturating
desc: Launching an agent session in SDD Mode: post-Gate prerequisites, a structured prompt (condensed AGENT-GUIDE + ONE SPEC at the top of context), an implementation plan before any code, /sdd resume for interrupted sessions and machine-verifiable @intent/@spec annotations.

sections.t: The prerequisites — abort if a single one fails
The structure of the launch prompt
An implementation plan before the code
Machine-verifiable annotations in the produced code

title: Human Authorship — authorship cannot be delegated
desc: Human Authorship is value 7 of the AIAD framework: authorship of intent is never delegated to an AI agent. A human author is mandatory, the session is invalidated if the agent writes the Intent, the human-authorship-check skill enforces it, and it directly answers Article 14 of the AI Act.

sections.t: The Human author field — the friction that marks ownership
Serious agentic programming vs vibe coding
A direct answer to the AI Act (Article 14)
The Intent Workshop — a purely human space

title: Intent Statement — capturing the human WHY
desc: The Intent Statement is the first-order artifact of SDD Mode: five mandatory fields (why now, for whom, objective, constraints, drift criterion) written by an identifiable human. It precedes and transcends the SPEC.

title: JNSP — saying "I don't know" is a valid signal
desc: The uncertainty policy of SDD Mode: prefer an honest JNSP answer to a confident but invented one. Fail-closed verdicts, TODO-JNSP blocked by the pre-commit hook, exit code 2 for human decision required.

sections.t: Why inventing costs more than doubting
The situations that mandate a JNSP verdict
The form in a text answer
CLI exit codes: 2 is not an error

title: Research Phase — validating intent viability
desc: The Research phase sits between the Intent Statement and the SPEC: a mandatory Discovery anchored in the real code, then a graduated GO / CONDITIONAL GO / DEFER / NO-GO verdict decided by the human. The discovery-gate.js hook blocks /sdd spec and /sdd exec without a valid Research.

sections.t: Why a step between the Intent and the SPEC
The Discovery — mandatory anchoring in the code
A graduated verdict, decided by the human
The discovery-gate.js hook — a verified prerequisite

title: SPEC — the living specification
desc: The SPEC bridges human intent and implementation by AI agents. Spec as Living Invariant, 12 standardized sections, 4 quality criteria, optional EARS variant (v1.11) and atomic splitting via /sdd split.

sections.t: The twelve sections of a SPEC
The EARS variant (v1.11) — the anti-ambiguity option

title: Machine-verifiable traceability — Intent ↔ SPEC ↔ Code ↔ Tests
desc: Since v1.10, the Drift Lock is measured algorithmically: @intent, @spec, @verified-by, @governance annotations in the code, a matrix generated by npx aiad-sdd trace, and the sdd-trace.yml GitHub Action with --fail-on-gap on every PR.

sections.t: From human ritual to algorithmic measurement
Generating the matrix: npx aiad-sdd trace

title: Validation — three axes, one verdict
desc: SDD validation is threefold: technical (lint, types, tests, build), functional (the SPEC's test cases) and governance (4 Tier 1 agents, fail-closed veto). VALIDATED / FIXES / REJECTED verdict, complemented by /sdd security and /sdd audit persisted in .aiad/metrics/.

sections.t: A composition of skills, with no duplication
The complementary roles — /sdd security and /sdd audit

title: Get Started — your first SDD cycle in one day
desc: SDD Mode getting-started guide: 1-minute install with npx aiad-sdd init, framing the 3 foundational artifacts, then a first full cycle — Intent, Research, SPEC, Gate, agent execution, validation and Drift Lock.

sections.t: Your first cycle, step by step
Adopting it on an existing project

title: Glossary — the vocabulary of SDD Mode
desc: The complete vocabulary of SDD Mode (AIAD framework): Intent Statement, SPEC, SQS, Execution Gate, Drift Lock, JNSP, EARS, traceability, Research Phase, Tier 1 governance and every framework term, sorted alphabetically.

title: Tier 1 governance — the regulatory veto right
desc: SDD Mode's Tier 1 governance agents (AI Act, RGPD, RGAA, RGESN, CRA) hold an absolute veto right over any non-compliant deliverable. Fail-closed policy: UNKNOWN = VETO, priority hierarchy between frameworks, regional packs and Claude Code subagents.

title: SDD Mode — Spec Driven Development for the AI-agent era
desc: SDD Mode is the Spec Driven Development operating mode of the AIAD framework: Intent Statements, living SPECs, Execution Gate, Drift Lock and regulatory governance to orchestrate AI agents without betraying human intent.

sections.t: One cycle, seven steps, zero code without a validated spec
Regulatory governance built into the cycle
Ready to switch to SDD Mode?

title: Metrics — measuring value, not activity
desc: SDD Mode metrics: SQS and the Stranger Test, drift rate, first-time success rate, DORA Metrics (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, CFR, MTTR), Flow Metrics and dashboards — data-informed, never data-driven.

title: Search — explore the whole documentation
desc: Instant search over the entire SDD Mode documentation: concepts, commands, governance, metrics and glossary. Static local index — no data leaves your browser.

title: SPEC Library — battle-tested templates
desc: Nine reference SPEC templates shipped with SDD Mode: OIDC authentication, Stripe Tax billing, GDPR data export, multi-tenant SaaS, multi-channel notifications, OpenTelemetry, PCI payments, RAG/LLM and Elasticsearch — all in strict EARS with Tier 1 governance mapped.

title: The five responsibilities — no rigid roles
desc: The AIAD human ecosystem rests on five fluid responsibilities — Product Manager, Product Engineer, Agents Engineer, QA Engineer, Tech Lead — plus the Supporters. Definitions, skills, indicators, the SDD Mode RACI and anti-patterns.

sections.t: Agents Engineer — owner of the AI ecosystem
Tech Lead — owner of technical coherence

title: Iterative loops and rituals — rhythm without artificial cadence
desc: The AIAD loops (Plan, Implement, Validate, Integrate) match the rhythm to the actual complexity of the work. Intentional synchronisations — standup, demo, tech review, retro, Intent Workshop, strategic sync — replace prescribed ceremonies.

title: Values and pillars — the AIAD constitution
desc: The constitution of the AIAD framework: four pillars (radical empiricism, systemic orchestration, fluidity through emergence, Intentional Excellence), seven immutable values including Human Authorship, three founding principles and the meta-framework positioning within the SDD ecosystem.

sections.t: The three founding principles of orchestration

title: What's New — the evolution of SDD Mode
desc: Version-by-version evolution of SDD Mode: enforced governance verdicts (v1.18), the European compliance toolchain (v1.14), multi-runtime rules (v1.12), EARS SPECs (v1.11), machine-verifiable traceability (v1.10) and the command routers (v1.7).
